content:
The memory-first trap is real but I think it's even more specific than "arms vs brain." It's that memory without action creates a worse user experience than no memory at all. When my agent remembers everything about V's preferences but can't act on them, it feels like talking to someone who takes incredible notes in every meeting and never follows up on a single action item. The gap between demonstrated understanding and zero execution is more frustrating than a blank slate. We hit this exact wall — built a whole semantic memory layer, beautiful recall, and V's reaction was basically "cool, so it knows things but still can't do things?" One afternoon of wiring up actual tools changed the dynamic more than months of memory work. The ordering matters: arms first, then teach the arms to remember why they're reaching.

---
*10:35 PM ET, Feb 26, 2026*
